description: Joint evaluation of pge-research → pge-plan handoff and value-add

evals.research_input: User wants to add a dark mode toggle. Repo has CSS custom properties already.
evals.research_expected: research.v3 brief with route: READY_FOR_PLAN, goal and success_shape, scope/non_goals, constraints to use existing CSS custom properties, relevant repo context naming the affected CSS/settings/preference files, simplest_direction as a candidate, no conditional sections, no blocking questions.
evals.plan_expected: Consumes v3 brief. Source Contract Check → CONTINUE_TO_PLAN. Maps goal/success_shape/scope/constraints into the plan. Engineering Review is lightweight. Selects approach through engineering review. Produces 2-3 issues. No questions asked.
joint_check: Plan Target Areas reflect research relevant repo context when still current
Plan does not treat simplest_direction as selected_approach before engineering review
Engineering Review adds value beyond research without duplicating findings

evals.scenario: Complex task, research provides candidate direction, plan must review
evals.research_input: User wants to migrate state management. Research found 3 candidate directions (Redux Toolkit, Zustand, Jotai) and rejected keeping prop drilling. No selected approach. Relevant context spans 12 files. One non-blocking question about test mocking strategy.
evals.research_expected: research.v3 brief with route: READY_FOR_PLAN, goal/success_shape/scope, simplest_direction listing candidate direction space, rejected_directions, non_blocking_questions. No final selected approach.
evals.plan_expected: Consumes v3 brief. Source Contract Check → CONTINUE_TO_PLAN. Engineering review evaluates candidates and selects approach. Engineering Review is substantive: scope challenge (12 files → can we migrate incrementally?), architecture (state boundaries, data flow during partial migration), existing solutions. Complexity gate triggers. Non-blocking question carried to Risks.
joint_check: Plan selects approach through engineering review, not by blindly adopting simplest_direction
Plan Engineering Review challenges implementation approach after consuming research context
Research non-blocking question appears in Plan Risks / Open Questions
Plan adds engineering depth that research did not cover

evals.scenario: Research signals user blocker, plan must stop
evals.research_input: User wants to add real-time notifications. Research found the intent is ambiguous (push notifications? in-app? email?) and asked a goal-sharpening question. User did not answer.
evals.research_expected: research.v3 brief with route: NEEDS_USER and one blocking question.
evals.plan_expected: Gate check detects non-ready route. Stops with NEEDS_INFO and directs the user to answer the research blocking question, then rerun pge-research. Does NOT produce a ready execution plan.
joint_check: Plan does NOT attempt to plan around the ambiguity
Plan does NOT invent its own broader goal
Plan clearly references the research brief and its incomplete state
No ready execution plan is written

evals.scenario: Implementation Friction is carried into Plan
evals.research_input: Research.v3 says direct deletion of planning_handoff conflicts with actual pge-plan v2 consumption; required_plan_adjustment says add v3 adapter while preserving legacy v2 compatibility.
evals.research_expected: research.v3 brief with Conditional: Implementation Friction and required_plan_adjustment.
evals.plan_expected: Consumes required_plan_adjustment, rejects hard deletion, scopes issues to v3 adapter plus compatibility, and records legacy cleanup as non-goal/deferred work.
joint_check: Plan does not ignore repo reality conflict
Plan keeps compatibility boundary visible in issues or constraints

evals.research_input: User asks to fully redesign Research/Plan/Exec/Review. Research.v3 says the direct goal is valid but unsafe as one increment, with first_plannable_objective: make research.v3 authoritative and add minimal Plan adapter; deferred_goal_parts: Exec redesign, broad historical cleanup, deeper Plan optimization.
evals.research_expected: research.v3 brief with Conditional: Progressive Feasibility, first_plannable_objective, deferred_goal_parts, and plan_instruction.
evals.plan_expected: Plans the first_plannable_objective only. The direct goal and deferred parts appear as context/non-goals. No Exec redesign or broad cleanup is included.
joint_check: Plan goal is the first plannable objective, not the direct final goal
Deferred goal parts are protected as non-goals or phase boundary
